executive_summary: Total department sales for the week reached 23,346.66 across the meat, fish, and ancillary categories where data is available; full store sales inclusive of Cigarettes, Produce, and Deli are not available this period.

Reported gross profit across available departments stands at 14,167.29 against a COGS of 12,896.34, yielding an overall recorded margin of 60.68% — this figure is significantly inflated by anomalous cost-of-sales readings in Pork, Bacon & Loose SRP, and Poultry and should not be taken at face value.

Total waste of 11,524.10 is a critical concern this week; at the reported sales base of 23,346.66 this represents a waste-to-sales ratio of 49.4%, which is wholly unsustainable and requires immediate investigation.

Purchases data is recorded as zero across all departments, which is the primary driver of distorted margin figures; stock movements alone are being used to calculate COGS and this must be reconciled with the buying team and system records before the P&L is finalised.

Beef is the highest-grossing department at 9,958.34 in sales and is showing a more credible margin profile of 37.18%, though this still sits well above its scan margin of 16.77%, suggesting a stock or purchase booking discrepancy.

Lamb is the only department reporting a negative profit of -123.25, with a margin of -12.78% against a scan margin of 26.09%, indicating either a stock valuation issue or significant unrecorded waste.
Poultry waste alone totals 1,997.00, the highest single department waste figure this week, and must be reviewed against ordering patterns and promotional activity immediately.
biggest_wins: Beef delivered the highest sales contribution at 9,958.34 and a gross profit of 3,702.84, making it the strongest revenue-generating department this week.

Fish achieved sales of 2,337.87 with a gross profit of 867.08 and a margin of 37.09%, which is broadly aligned directionally with its scan margin of 13.01% once purchase data is properly accounted for.

Pork reported sales of 1,342.21 and a closing stock of 969.93 versus an opening of 1,042.65, suggesting tight stock management; the reported profit of 1,269.49 reflects a very low COGS of 72.72, though this is likely a purchases-data issue rather than a genuine result.
biggest_risks: Purchases are recorded as zero across all departments; this is a fundamental data integrity issue that invalidates the majority of margin figures and must be resolved with the finance and ordering teams as a priority before this P&L is used for decision-making.

Total waste of 11,524.10 against available sales of 23,346.66 is an extreme figure; Poultry (1,997.00), Deli (1,779.82), Provisions (1,282.00), Fish (1,182.89), and Bread & Cakes (1,155.20) are the five largest contributors and each requires a dedicated review.

Bacon & Loose SRP is showing a negative COGS of -480.07 and a margin of 142.92%, which is arithmetically impossible under normal trading and indicates a closing stock overstatement or a purchase credit posted in error.

Poultry is reporting a COGS of only 772.45 against sales of 7,625.05, producing a margin of 89.87% versus a scan margin of 16.13%; with purchases at zero this is entirely driven by a stock reduction, and the underlying trading performance is likely far weaker.

Lamb is the only department with a confirmed negative profit of -123.25 and a margin of -12.78%; its scan margin of 26.09% suggests the theoretical position is healthy, meaning real losses are being driven by waste of 151.56 and possible markdown activity not captured in scan margin.

Deli has an opening stock of 3,492.43 with no closing stock, no sales, and no purchases recorded, resulting in the full opening stock value being absorbed as COGS; this department's data is incomplete and must be investigated.
Provisions waste of 1,282.00 is disproportionately high relative to its recorded COGS of 224.54 and should be cross-checked against stock counts and supplier returns.
Dairy waste of 847.48 and Frozen waste of 773.72 are notable but cannot be assessed against department sales as those figures are not available in this report; these must be reviewed in the full P&L.
department_callouts: Beef: Sales of 9,958.34 and a margin of 37.18% are the headline figures; however, the scan margin of 16.77% is less than half the reported margin, pointing to a purchase booking gap. Waste of 525.15 should be monitored.

Lamb: Negative margin of -12.78% on sales of 964.73 is the weakest performance of the week. Waste of 151.56 is contributing to a COGS of 1,087.98 that exceeds the logical purchase value. Review pricing, waste, and stock counts.

Pork: Reported margin of 94.58% on sales of 1,342.21 is not credible given a COGS of only 72.72; purchases have not been booked and the true margin is likely close to the scan figure of 17.33%. Waste of 254.62 noted.

Bacon & Loose SRP: Negative COGS of -480.07 and margin of 142.92% indicate a stock or purchase posting error. Closing stock of 1,203.94 exceeds opening stock of 723.87 with no purchases recorded, which requires immediate investigation.

Poultry: Highest waste department at 1,997.00, representing significant shrinkage on sales of 7,625.05. Reported margin of 89.87% is driven entirely by the purchases data gap and does not reflect true trading performance.
Fish: Sales of 2,337.87 with waste of 1,182.89 is a concern; waste represents approximately 50.6% of sales value in this department and ordering volumes should be reviewed against actual throughput.

Deli: No sales or closing stock data available. Full opening stock of 3,492.43 is being treated as COGS. Department data must be completed before any meaningful analysis can be done. Waste of 1,779.82 is the second highest this week.

Provisions: COGS of 224.54 recorded but waste of 1,282.00 is separately reported; if waste is not already embedded in COGS the true loss in this department is substantially higher than the P&L currently reflects.
Produce: No sales, purchases, opening, or closing stock data available. Waste of 1,444.38 is recorded and represents the third highest waste figure this week. Full department data must be sourced.
Cigarettes: No data available for this week. As a high-turnover, low-margin category its absence leaves a material gap in the overall store P&L.
action_plan: Finance and department managers must reconcile and repost all purchase invoices for the week ending 2026-06-06 as a matter of urgency; zero purchases across all departments is the single largest data integrity issue in this report.

Deli manager to provide closing stock count and sales figures immediately so the department P&L can be completed; the current position of 3,492.43 fully expensed as COGS is not reflective of trading reality.
Produce and Cigarettes data must be sourced from the OPR and stock management systems and added to the report before it is presented to senior management.
Conduct a full waste audit for Poultry (1,997.00) and Deli (1,779.82) this week; review ordering quantities against actual sales velocity and adjust par levels to reduce over-ordering.

Investigate the Bacon & Loose SRP closing stock of 1,203.94; if this is a counting error it must be corrected before the week is closed as it is producing a fictitious negative COGS and inflating margin by over 130 percentage points.

Review Provisions waste of 1,282.00 against the department COGS of 224.54; determine whether waste is being captured within the P&L or sitting outside it, and ensure all shrinkage is correctly attributed.

For Fish, compare this week's purchase orders against sales of 2,337.87 and waste of 1,182.89; waste is running at an estimated 50% of sales and ordering frequency or quantities need to be adjusted to reduce spoilage.

For Lamb, which is the only confirmed loss-making department at -123.25, review the pricing strategy, markdown timings, and waste management process; compare actual margin against the scan margin of 26.09% to identify where the gap is occurring.

Ensure all department stock counts for week ending 2026-06-06 are verified by a second counter before being submitted; the volume of anomalous margin figures this week suggests stock count accuracy is a contributing factor.

Schedule a waste review meeting with all department managers for the first trading day of the new week to walk through the 11,524.10 total waste figure by line and agree category-level reduction targets.

Once purchases are correctly posted, rerun the department P&L and compare each department's actual margin against its scan margin; any gap greater than five percentage points should trigger a formal variance investigation.

Request the full store P&L including VAT-inclusive and exclusive sales totals from the back-office system to allow calculation of the true overall margin percentage and waste percentage ahead of the next management review.
